title: boronia tetrandra var. pilosa
intro: Boronia tetrandra var. pilosa, also known as the hairy brown boronia, is a flowering plant native to Australia, belonging to the family Rutaceae. The hairy brown boronia has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its ability to relieve coughs, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a skin toner and antiseptic to treat wounds and minor injuries. The hairy brown boronia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile and valuable addition to traditional herbal medicine.
